title: ORB works when restricted to instruments with abnormal relative volume (Zarattini-Barbon-Aziz)
claim: Opening range breakout profitability concentrates in 'stocks in play' — instruments with unusually high trading activity that day due to fundamental news; relative volume measured in the opening range positively correlates with subsequent breakout P&L, and restricting ORB trades to the highest relative-volume names is what makes the strategy profitable net of costs.

source.label: Zarattini, Barbon & Aziz (2024), 'A Profitable Day Trading Strategy For The U.S. Equity Market', SSRN 4729284 (authors' summary page)
source.quote: These are stocks experiencing unusually high trading activity due to company-specific news, earnings announcements, or other fundamental developments.

rule_formal: RelVol = volume in first 5 minutes today / average first-5-minute volume over prior days; rank universe by RelVol at open; trade 5-minute ORB (long above OR high / short below OR low) only on top-ranked names; stop and EOD exit per paper.

numbers.effect: Top-20 stocks-in-play portfolio: >1,600% total net return, Sharpe 2.81, annualized alpha 36% (vs S&P 500 ~198% same period); beta ~0
numbers.caveats: Equities cross-section with name-selection dimension gold does not have; numbers from authors' own summary page of the SSRN paper (SSRN itself 403); working paper, not peer-reviewed journal

mechanism: A fundamental catalyst forces repricing: news-driven participants must build/liquidate positions within the day, generating persistent one-directional flow after the opening range resolves; without a catalyst (normal volume) the breakout has no forced flow behind it and mean-reverts
relevance: Q2 directly: the condition under which ORB works is abnormal participation, measured as relative volume during range formation; for XAUUSD the analog is tick-count/volume of the Asian range or of the breakout bar relative to trailing same-hour average — a realtime Q1 discriminator candidate that is NOT among the five falsified day-level price variables

test_design.lookahead_risk: Relative volume computable at range end (08:00) or at breakout bar from M1 tick-counts vs trailing same-hour baseline — fully known at entry

overlap_risk: Volume proxies correlate with realized volatility, so partial overlap with dead rv (prev-day vol percentile); key difference: rv was previous-day price volatility, this is same-day participation measured before/at entry — untested dimension (tick-count never used in the five falsified variables)
